mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and economic momentum, which triggers short-term bullish sentiment and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal loses statistical significance or reverses direction during early-cycle expansion periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when inflation crosses the 3.5 percent threshold, invalidating the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates aggressive labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending to drive positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect fails to improve model fit, with the combined proxy signal underperforming independent single-series predictors in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth confirms robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth data shows no correlation with forward equity returns during expansion regimes, with an information coefficient consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production greater than 1 percent signals immediate supply chain disruptions or demand shocks, triggering rapid portfolio rebalancing and negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The market rallies or remains flat following industrial production drops exceeding 1 percent, with positive returns occurring in over 55 percent of triggered cases.
